title: Patricia A. Jackson

{{Quote|As a Black woman, I grew up with a genre where the heroes never looked like me. So I decided to create a world where my ethnic culture could be expanded on and enriched. That world was Socorro, which depicted the struggle of the indigenous people who were just trying to eke out a living without interference. The story is an old one. Corellian colonists came to claim the planet, but were themselves claimed and became part of the culture.|Patricia A. Jackson|<ref name="Star Wars Interview">{{WebCite|url=https://starwarsinterviews.com/various/authors/patricia-a-jackson-author/|text=Patricia A. Jackson Author|work=Star Wars Interviews|author=Dennis Pellegrom|archivedate=20220428102231}}</ref>}}
'''Patricia A. Jackson''' is an American author. She wrote a number of short ''[[Star Wars]]'' stories in the 1990s for the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'', mainly focused on original characters, such as the [[Socorran]] smuggler [[Drake Paulsen]], and perhaps most famously, Imperial Inquisitor [[Adalric Cessius Brandl]]. She created the first words and phrases of the [[Olys Corellisi/Legends|Old Corellian language]] for the Drake Paulsen stories and expanded on the language in an article, "[[Old Corellian: A Guide for the Curious Scholar]]." Jackson also wrote the [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[The Black Sands of Socorro]]''.

A further story called ''[[Emanations of Darkness]]'', continuing the story of Adalric Brandl, his son [[Jaalib Brandl|Jaalib]], and Rebel operative [[Fable Astin/Legends|Fable Astin]], was written for ''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' but eventually appeared online at the [http://www.swrpgnetwork.com SWRPGNetwork] website. 

Jackson wrote a [[canon]] short story titled "[[Gone to the Winner's Circle]]," which was published as part of the [[2023]] anthology book ''[[From a Certain Point of View: Return of the Jedi]]''.
